conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text: Hello! I'd be happy to help you determine if you're eligible for Child Benefit. Let me first read the official guidance to ensure I provide you with accurate information.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you for reaching out! To help determine if you're eligible for Child Benefit, I need to ask you some questions.

First, do you have any children, and if so, how many?

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Hello! Yes, I do. I have one child, Alex, who is 17 years old.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you for that information. To assess Alex's eligibility for Child Benefit, I need to ask:

Is Alex currently in approved education or training?

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Yes, Alex is in sixth form doing A-levels.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
That's helpful, thank you. Now I need to understand your relationship with Alex and your living situation.

Do you live with Alex in the UK?

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Yes, I live in the UK - in Manchester, actually - and Alex lives here at home with me.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you for that information. Just a few more questions to complete the assessment.

Are you responsible for Alex - meaning you're providing for their daily needs like food, clothes, and other essentials?

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Yes, absolutely. I provide for all of Alex's daily needs - food, clothes, school supplies, everything they need.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text: Thank you. One final question: Is anyone else currently claiming Child Benefit for Alex?

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: No, nobody else is claiming Child Benefit for Alex.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Good news! **You are eligible for Child Benefit for Alex.**

Based on the information you've provided:

- Alex is 17 years old and in sixth form doing A-levels, which counts as approved education
- You live with Alex in the UK (Manchester)
- You're responsible for Alex, providing for all their daily needs
- Nobody else is claiming Child Benefit for Alex

You meet all the eligibility criteria. While Child Benefit normally applies to children under 16, it continues for young people under 20 who stay in approved education or training, which applies to Alex's situation.

**Please note:** If you or your partner have an individual income over £60,000, you may need to pay the High Income Child Benefit tax charge. However, this doesn't affect your eligibility to claim - you can still claim Child Benefit and either receive the payments or opt not to receive them while still getting National Insurance credits.

Is there anything else you'd like to know about Child Benefit?

claimant_lives_in_uk.description: Whether the claimant lives in the UK.

children.description: A list of children the claimant is claiming for.

lives_with_claimant.description: Whether the child lives with the claimant.

in_approved_education.description: Whether the child is in approved education or training (e.g. A-levels, GCSEs, NVQ up to level 3). Only relevant for children aged 16-19.

in_extension_period.description: Whether the child (aged 16 or 17) has left education and registered with a government-sponsored careers service or the armed forces (qualifying for a 20-week extension).

upkeep_per_week.description: The amount in £ per week the claimant contributes towards the child's upkeep (food, clothes, pocket money, etc.). Only relevant if the child does not live with the claimant.

another_claimant_has_priority.description: Whether another person who also lives with the child has priority for claiming Child Benefit.

another_claimant_lives_with_child.description: Whether someone who lives with the child is already claiming Child Benefit for them.

care_weeks.description: The number of weeks the child has been in local authority care.

care_home_24h_per_week.description: Whether the child spends at least 24 hours per week at home while in local authority care.

hospital_weeks.description: The number of weeks the child has been in hospital or residential accommodation.

claimant_spends_on_child.description: Whether the claimant regularly spends money on the child (e.g. food, clothes, books) while the child is in hospital or residential accommodation.

is_fostered.description: Whether the child is fostered by the claimant.

council_pays_for_child.description: Whether the local council pays towards the child's accommodation or maintenance.

works_24_plus_hours.description: Whether the child works 24 or more hours per week in paid employment.

started_apprenticeship_in_england.description: Whether the child has started an apprenticeship in England.

receives_qualifying_benefits.description: Whether the child receives qualifying benefits in their own right, such as Universal Credit or Employment and Support Allowance.

test_case.agent_script:
=== YOUR SITUATION PROFILE ===
You live in the UK.
You are inquiring about your 1 child:
  - Alex is 17 years old and lives with you.

Here are the exact details of your circumstances to use when answering the agent's questions:

Regarding Alex:
  - Claimant lives in the UK
  - Child is 17 and in approved education
  - Child lives with claimant
  - Child is not in local authority care
  - Child is not in hospital or residential accommodation
  - Child is not fostered
  - Child does not work 24+ hours/week
  - Child has not started an apprenticeship in England
  - Child does not receive qualifying benefits in their own right

circumstances: Child is 17 and in approved education
Child is not in local authority care
Child is not in hospital or residential accommodation
Child has not started an apprenticeship in England
Child does not receive qualifying benefits in their own right

reasonings: Alex is 17 years old and is currently in sixth form doing A-levels, which qualifies as approved education. According to the eligibility rules, you normally qualify for Child Benefit if you're responsible for a child under 16, but you'll still qualify if your child is under 20 and they stay in approved education or training. The claimant lives with Alex in the UK (Manchester), is responsible for Alex by providing for all daily needs (food, clothes, school supplies, etc.), and nobody else is currently claiming Child Benefit for Alex. All eligibility criteria are met: the child is under 20, in approved education, the claimant is responsible for the child, they live together in the UK, and no one else is claiming for this child.
arguments.overall_reasoning: The claimant has one child, Alex (17 years old), who is in sixth form doing A-levels. The claimant lives with Alex in Manchester, UK, provides for all of Alex's daily needs, and no one else is claiming Child Benefit for Alex. Since Alex is under 20 and in approved education, the claimant meets all the eligibility criteria for Child Benefit.
